title: Ryan Fox Wins 2026 Open Championship, First Major, With Final-Hole Birdie
dek: New Zealander Ryan Fox won the 2026 Open Championship at Royal Birkdale, his first major title, sealed with a birdie on the final hole, according to BBC Sport.
author: The GoCheckMySports Desk
key_fact: Ryan Fox won the 2026 Open Championship at Royal Birkdale, his first major, with a final-hole birdie, per BBC Sport.
bottom_line: Watch for BBC Sport's full tournament recap to confirm Fox's final score, the runner-up and margin, and the exact circumstances of the closing birdie. Those details would complete the picture of how narrow or comfortable this first major title actually was.
body: Ryan Fox won the 2026 Open Championship at Royal Birkdale Golf Club, according to BBC Sport, the first major title of his career. The victory was sealed with a birdie on the final hole, per the same reporting.

For readers following major championship golf, this is the headline result: a New Zealander closing out a first major on the last hole, a breakthrough moment BBC Sport's coverage frames around that closing shot.
BBC Sport confirms Fox as the champion, identifies him as a New Zealander, and states this marks his first major win, with the deciding birdie coming on the 72nd hole.

Winning a major with a birdie on the final hole typically means the outcome was in question until the last shot, whether to secure outright victory or avoid a playoff, though the available reporting does not specify which scenario applied here.

The source material does not include Fox's final round score, his overall tournament total relative to par or the field, who finished second, or the margin of victory. No quotes from Fox or his competitors appear in the available reporting.

What is confirmed, per BBC Sport: Fox's win, the venue, the first-major distinction, and the final-hole birdie. The scorecard details and the runner-up picture remain to be filled in by fuller reporting.
